page: Microsoft Azure FX16-8mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmdsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with a clock speed of 4.2 GHz, 4 physical cores, and 2 threads per core. The processor is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, alongside hardware support for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX, AVX-512), Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), and cryptography acceleration. The server is configured with 336.0 GB of system memory, providing 42.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 945 GB of local storage. No GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2165.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 34.31 GB/sec. Text processing is highly efficient, achieving a top-tier Geekbench text processing score of 2931.0. However, memory sub-system benchmarks show some constraints. The Passmark memory score is categorized as weak at 1324.99, and large-block memory read bandwidth drops to 44.97 GB/sec compared to the cached small-block read speed of 691.15 GB/sec. Multi-core performance remains in the average tier, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 8882.55 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 7546.0.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX16-8mds_v2 offers high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it a specialized option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling. The inclusion of 945 GB of local storage and premium storage capabilities supports data-heavy operations. This server is suited for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching layers (achieving 12.07 million SET operations per second in benchmarks), software compilation, and moderate-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure FX16-8mds_v2 is a memory-optimized x86_64 server from the FXmdsv2 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs via an Intel Xeon 8573C processor clocked at 4.2 GHz, 4 physical cores, and a 260 MB L3 cache. The server is equipped with 336.0 GB of RAM (42.0 GB per core) and 945 GB of local storage, with no bundled GPUs. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, including a Geekbench single-core score of 2165.0 and top-tier text processing. However, memory performance is mixed, with a weak Passmark memory score of 1324.99 despite high capacity. This high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads rather than compute-heavy scaling. It is suited for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX16-8mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 336 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage, suited for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX16-8mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 336 GB RAM, and 945 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A high-memory, storage-equipped instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors designed for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
336.0 GB of RAM providing an exceptionally high 42.0 GB per core ratio
945 GB of local storage with premium storage capabilities
Strong single-core performance and top-tier text processing capabilities
Suited for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching, and software compilation
